title: ProtocolLab Secure DNS Performance Lane Isolation
purpose: Define secure DNS transport lanes as independent performance and proof domains while permitting shared deterministic DNS semantics.
scope: This slice records lane identity and comparison isolation for DNS over TLS, DNS over HTTP/2, DNS over HTTP/3, and DNS over QUIC. Detailed wire fixtures, profiles, suites, executors, and implementation packages remain a later contract slice.

requirements.title: Independent secure DNS lanes
requirements.statement: ProtocolLab MUST treat DoT, DoH over HTTP/2, DoH over HTTP/3, and DoQ as four independent execution, protocol-proof, executor-identity, and comparison lanes.

requirements.title: Shared deterministic DNS semantics
requirements.statement: The four secure DNS lanes MAY share one deterministic local DNS question-and-answer fixture when each lane independently proves its exact transport binding and validates the same DNS message semantics.

requirements.title: Secure DNS fallback rejection
requirements.statement: A secure DNS lane MUST reject execution through another DNS transport, another HTTP version, raw QUIC, a public recursive resolver, or an uncontrolled upstream lookup as evidence for the requested lane.
